reforms.description: The new legislation on industrial subsidies, effective January 1998, prioritizes support for research, product development, and training, marking a shift towards intangible investments in Finland's industrial policy. Additionally, the establishment of the 'Advisory Committee on Business Subsidies' aims to enhance the effectiveness of future support programmes while limiting public support to the manufacturing industry, ensuring that government measures correct market failures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance support for R&D and product development, which are critical for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation was effective from January 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in Finland's industrial policy towards supporting intangible investments.
reforms.source_quote: The new legislation on general conditions for the provision of industrial subsidies, which entered into force in January 1998, states that support schemes shall be primarily targeted on research, product development, training or on some other form of immaterial corporate development.

reforms.description: In 1997, Mk 3 billion was allocated to universities, research institutes, and private companies to foster R&D activities, reflecting a significant increase in public funding for research. The shift from sector-specific and enterprise-specific supports towards horizontal policy areas in Finland indicates a significant policy reform aimed at broadening the scope of public support to include regional development, R&D, and SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in public funding for R&D is expected to enhance research capabilities and innovation across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding allocation was made in 1997 and the shift in policy was noted in 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant but narrower change in the allocation of public funding for R&D.
reforms.source_quote: In 1997, Mk 3 billion was allocated to universities, research institutes and private companies, mostly through programmes by the Technology Development Centre of the Ministry of Trade and Industry (TEKES).

reforms.description: The Finnish government plans to merge three institutions providing industrial support, namely KERA, the Finnish Guarantee Board, and Finfund, to enhance their operational effectiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the merger on innovation and economic growth is uncertain until the new structure is operational.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan to merge the institutions has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The merger is an administrative change aimed at improving efficiency rather than a significant policy reform.
reforms.source_quote: Part of the industrial support takes the form of soft loans by KERA (the Industrial Investment Fund; directed towards SMEs), the Finnish Guarantee Board (export guarantees) and Finfund (development aid). These three institutions will be merged in order to improve their operations.
